$schema: Declarative config→doc drift map. Each entry: when any file matching `paths` changes in a diff range, at least ONE file in `docs` must change too, or the gate fails with `why`. Consumed by tools/scripts/config_doc_check.py (mirrors skill_sync_check.py). Bypass on the tip commit with `Config-Doc: skip reason="..."`.

entries.why: Shipyard validation config (targets, gates, routing) is documented in the versioning + local-ci guides; a change here without a doc edit silently drifts the operator-facing description of how CI validates and merges.

The Shipyard binary pin and installer are covered by the versioning guide's three-layer enforcement design; bumping the pin or changing install logic without a doc edit drifts the documented toolchain.
The build workflow's runner routing (self-hosted macOS + GitHub-hosted lanes) is documented in local-ci.md; changing job/runner wiring without a doc edit drifts the documented CI topology.

The routing contract IS the documented lane->label topology; local-ci.md was already drifted from the live variables (it described the macOS overflow lane as pointing at a label the variable had not named for months) precisely because the two could move independently. Changing a lane without updating the guide re-opens that gap.
The auto-release + watchdog + cadence-check trio is the release-watchdog design; changing any of them without a doc edit drifts the documented three-layer release-failure protection.

The off-fleet queue-age watchdog's thresholds and its two-condition alarm rule (age AND lane-liveness) are documented in local-ci.md, including the measured queue-age baseline they are calibrated against. Retuning the guard without a doc edit drifts the operator-facing account of when CI pages a human — and a monitor whose documented behavior is wrong gets muted.
The authoritative version/skill/coverage gate workflows are described by the versioning guide; changing the enforced gates without a doc edit drifts the documented enforcement layer.

The single-writer post-merge version-assignment bot is documented by the cutover guide, including the dry-run→--push flip and the one-time straggler rule; changing the workflow (especially the flip off dry-run) without a doc edit drifts the release-critical cutover procedure.
